claims.claim_text: Learners may encounter openly documented models that still require approval or terms before access.
claims.span_hint: section: Open Is Not One Thing
claims.rationale: Hugging Face documents gated models that have public model pages but still require access requests or approval before files can be downloaded.

claims.claim_text: Model cards on a model hub typically carry intended use, limitations, license metadata, and information about derivative relationships such as fine-tunes or quantized versions.
claims.span_hint: section: What A Model Hub Actually Does
claims.rationale: The Hugging Face Model Cards documentation directly describes intended uses, limitations, license metadata, and base-model relationships including fine-tuned and quantized variants.

claims.claim_text: Gemma family: strong Google-backed open-model ecosystem, now including Gemma 4.
claims.span_hint: section: Examples Of Model Families You Will Encounter
claims.anchor_text: strong Google-backed open-model ecosystem, now including Gemma 4
claims.suggested_rewrite: a Google-backed model family worth comparing alongside other open-model options
claims.rationale: Google's official Gemma documentation shows released Gemma families such as Gemma 3 and Gemma 3n, but not a supported 'Gemma 4' claim, so the version-specific statement should be removed.

claims.claim_text: Llama family: widely used reference point in open-model discussion.
claims.span_hint: section: Examples Of Model Families You Will Encounter
claims.anchor_text: widely used reference point in open-model discussion
claims.suggested_rewrite: another model family to include in side-by-side comparisons
claims.rationale: This is a popularity judgment without a defined benchmark or source, so it should be rewritten as neutral instructional guidance.

claims.claim_text: Qwen family: strong multilingual and practical deployment interest.
claims.span_hint: section: Examples Of Model Families You Will Encounter
claims.anchor_text: strong multilingual and practical deployment interest
claims.suggested_rewrite: a model family worth checking when multilingual support matters to your use case
claims.rationale: The line mixes a real capability area with an uncited qualitative judgment, so it should be reframed as comparison guidance rather than asserted as fact.

claims.claim_text: Mistral family: popular for efficient open-weight deployments.
claims.span_hint: section: Examples Of Model Families You Will Encounter
claims.suggested_rewrite: a model family worth checking when you care about smaller open-weight deployment options
claims.rationale: The bullet makes an unsupported popularity-and-efficiency judgment, so it should be rewritten to preserve the learning cue without the unbounded claim.

further_reading.title: The Model Hub
further_reading.why_relevant: Gives a primary-source overview of what a modern model hub is for and how model repositories are organized.

further_reading.title: Model Cards
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows exactly what information responsible learners should look for before downloading or deploying a model.

further_reading.title: Gated Models
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains why some public model pages still require approval, license acknowledgment, or extra access conditions.

notes: Most of the module is pedagogical framing. The citation-relevant material is concentrated in the model-hub mechanics and the named model-family examples, especially the incorrect Gemma 4 reference.
